questions.question: What does sns.set_theme(style='whitegrid') do?
options: Applies a visual theme globally -- whitegrid adds horizontal grid lines on white background
questions.explanation: sns.set_theme() sets defaults for all subsequent seaborn plots. Common styles: white, whitegrid, dark, darkgrid, ticks.

questions.question: sns.histplot vs sns.distplot:
options: histplot is the current API (seaborn v0.11+); distplot is deprecated -- use histplot for new code
questions.explanation: distplot was deprecated in v0.11. histplot with kde=True gives the same result plus better control.

questions.question: The hue parameter in seaborn plots:
options: Creates separate layers/colours for each category in the specified column
questions.explanation: hue='group' automatically creates separate coloured elements (bars, lines, violin) per group value.

questions.question: FacetGrid is used for:
options: Creating a grid of plots -- one panel per category level
Adding facets/panels to a single axis
questions.explanation: g = sns.FacetGrid(df, col='subject') creates separate subplot per subject. map() applies a plot function to each.

questions.question: sns.regplot adds to a scatter plot:
options: A fitted regression line with confidence interval shading
questions.explanation: regplot = scatter + linear regression line + 95% CI band. Quick way to visualise linear relationship.

questions.question: sns.heatmap with annot=True does what?
options: Writes the cell values as text inside each heatmap cell
questions.explanation: annot=True + fmt='.2f' writes formatted numbers inside cells. Essential for correlation matrices.

questions.question: The violin plot shows:
options: The full distribution shape (like a rotated KDE) + inner quartile stats
questions.explanation: Violin = KDE rotated + mirrored on both sides. inner='quartile' shows the IQR box inside.

questions.question: sns.boxplot with multiple hue levels:
options: Creates grouped box plots side by side per category -- hue adds a second grouping variable
questions.explanation: e.g. x='subject', hue='group' creates two boxes per subject (Control and Treatment) side by side.

questions.question: The confidence interval shown by default in sns.lineplot is:
options: 95% CI of the mean (not the data range) -- shows how confident we are in the mean estimate
questions.explanation: seaborn's default CI is 95% via bootstrapping. ci=None removes it. ci='sd' shows standard deviation instead.

questions.question: When should you use sns.violinplot instead of sns.boxplot?
options: When you want to see the full distribution shape, not just quartiles -- especially for bimodal distributions
When you have very few data points
questions.explanation: Boxplot shows 5 numbers. Violin shows the full distribution shape -- bimodal distributions are invisible in boxplots.
